fields.description:
## Why
The agent landing contract only works if agents adopt it: the old "watch the 2 votes" model is now broken (two green votes no longer means "it will land" ‚Äî a conflict blocks it while votes stay green). Docs must teach the new contract or the conflict case silently reintroduces babysitting.

## What
Rewrite this repo's landing section in `CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` to the new contract, and record the design in `docs/adr/0042-*.md`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) ‚Äî the `Autosubmit` label ‚Äî and **S4** (`brightish-growable-dodo`, 73f5-4df7-4fac-4738) ‚Äî the `land` command: the docs must not merge to `main` until the label and command they instruct agents to use are actually live ‚Äî otherwise the guide points at tooling that does not yet exist.

## Scope
Docs + ADR only.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `CLAUDE.md` + `CONTRIBUTING.md` landing section rewritten: "set `Autosubmit` / run `land`; act on the single typed outcome; do NOT watch votes or treat two green votes as 'it will land'."
- [ ] **Grep the whole repo** for the deprecated "two green votes" / "watch votes" pattern (e.g. in `docs/your-first-change.md`) and update **ALL** occurrences to the new contract ‚Äî not only `CLAUDE.md`/`CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- [ ] `docs/adr/0042-*.md` records the auto-lander design AND the rationale behind the key decisions, so future idea-epics inherit the "why", not just the "what". It MUST capture: the agent contract + outcome enum; the R5 safety invariant (tested tree = merged tree) and how each mechanism preserves it; the **eight key design decisions with their prior-art grounding** (Zuul / LUCI CV-Chromium CQ / Gerrit primitives / Prow-Tide) ‚Äî the `Autosubmit`‚Üî`CQ+2` label, the two-channel status surface, the CQ-reset hand-back, the raw time-to-land timer, the autoheal hang-recovery, the stdlib-not-pygerrit2 client choice, `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader`, and the sentinel+drain stop; the separation-of-concerns boundary (project infra, not `rebar` core); the reliability posture + accepted single-instance SPOF tradeoff; and the **deferrals with their promotion triggers** ‚Äî the provider-seam (idea `1701-8bc1-e3b7-4984`, promote on a 2nd landing platform), bounded-speculation batching (idea `c3a9-5f4c-bdc6-4e27`, promote only on MEASURED serial-treadmill pain), and HA/external-state (promote if single-instance availability is insufficient). **Confirm both idea ids resolve in the store** and anchor ADR-0042 to them. (A full drafted record is attached as a comment on this ticket ‚Äî land that content.)
- [ ] Docs cross-reference ADR-0040 (FFO) + ADR-0041 (LLM-Review `TRIVIAL_REBASE` carry) as the gate substrate the lander sits on.
